patient_medical_home.title: What is a Patient Medical Home?
patient_medical_home.subtitle: Discover how the Patient Medical Home model transforms healthcare delivery to provide comprehensive, coordinated, and patient-centered care.

understanding.title: Understanding the Patient Medical Home
understanding.content1: A Patient Medical Home (PMH) is a healthcare delivery model that provides comprehensive, coordinated, and patient-centered care. It serves as the central hub for all of a patient's healthcare needs, ensuring continuity, accessibility, and quality in care delivery.
understanding.content2: The PMH model emphasizes the importance of building strong relationships between patients and their healthcare providers, fostering trust, communication, and collaboration in the care process.

features.title: Key Features of the Patient Medical Home

comprehensive.title: Comprehensive Care
comprehensive.content: Addresses most of each patient's physical and mental healthcare needs, including prevention and wellness, acute care, and chronic care.

patient_centered.title: Patient-Centered
patient_centered.content: Provides healthcare that is respectful of and responsive to individual patient preferences, needs, and values.

coordinated.title: Coordinated Care
coordinated.content: Coordinates care across all elements of the broader healthcare system, including specialty care, hospitals, home healthcare, and community services.

accessible.title: Accessible Services
accessible.content: Offers accessible services with shorter waiting times for urgent needs, enhanced in-person hours, 24/7 telephone or electronic access to a member of the care team.

quality.title: Quality and Safety
quality.content: Committed to quality improvement and evidence-based medicine, using clinical decision support tools and participating in performance measurement and improvement.

team_based.title: Team-Based Care
team_based.content: Led by physicians, physician assistants, or nurse practitioners who work together with other healthcare professionals to provide comprehensive care.

benefits.title: Benefits of the Patient Medical Home
items: Reduced healthcare costs and hospital readmissions
Greater focus on prevention and wellness
Improved communication between patients and providers
Greater access to care and services
Greater emphasis on patient education and self-care

coordinator.title: Primary Care Provider as Coordinator
coordinator.content: The primary care provider serves as the central coordinator, ensuring that all aspects of patient care are managed and communicated appropriately across different healthcare settings.

team.title: Integrated Healthcare Team
team.content: A multidisciplinary team works together to provide comprehensive care, including physicians, nurses, specialists, pharmacists, and other healthcare professionals.

technology.title: Technology Integration
technology.content: Electronic health records and health information technology systems are used to facilitate communication, coordination, and continuity of care among all providers.

engagement.title: Patient Engagement
engagement.content: Patients actively participate in their care decisions, with emphasis on education, self-care, and shared decision-making processes.

cta.title: Experience Patient Medical Home Care
cta.content: Join our Patient Medical Home program and experience the benefits of comprehensive, coordinated, and patient-centered healthcare.

map.title: Location
map.button: View exact location on Google Maps

patient_rights.title: Patient Rights and Empowerment
patient_rights.subtitle: Learn about your rights as a patient and how to exercise them

rights.title: Patient Rights
items: Be treated with respect and dignity
Maintain confidentiality of your medical information

responsibilities.title: Patient Responsibilities
items: Respect other patients and medical staff
Report changes in your health condition

lead_screening.title: Lead Screening
lead_screening.subtitle: Important information about lead poisoning prevention

prevention.title: Lead Poisoning Prevention
prevention.content: Lead is a toxic metal that can cause serious health problems, especially in young children.

symptoms.title: Symptoms
symptoms.content: Symptoms may include learning, behavior, and growth problems.

prevention_tips.title: Prevention Tips
items: Keep the house clean and dust-free
Prevent children from playing in dirt
Eat foods rich in calcium and iron

overview.title: Program Description
overview.content: The VIP PCMH program offers comprehensive and coordinated medical care for our patients.
